qa_pairs.question: What types of database servers does the Alliance provide for researchers?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance offers access to MySQL and Postgres database servers for researchers.

qa_pairs.question: On which clusters are database servers available for researchers?
qa_pairs.answer: Database servers are available for researchers on both Cedar and Graham clusters.

qa_pairs.question: Will database servers be available on the Graham cluster after January 13, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: No, as of January 13, 2025, no database server will be provided on Graham during the transition to the new Nibi system.

qa_pairs.question: What is the operational status of the Graham cluster as of January 13, 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: As of January 13, 2025, the Graham cluster will be operating at approximately 25% capacity until Nibi is available.

qa_pairs.question: What is the general description of the Cedar MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar MySQL server is a general purpose server for researchers wanting to set up SQL tables and issue SQL commands against them.

qa_pairs.question: What is the full server name for Cedar MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: The full server name for Cedar MySQL is cedar-mysql-vm.int.cedar.computecanada.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the short server name for Cedar MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: The short server name for Cedar MySQL is cedar-mysql-vm.

qa_pairs.question: What is the latest MariaDB version available on the Cedar MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: MariaDB version 11.5 is the latest version available on the Cedar MySQL server.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find documentation for the Cedar MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation for the Cedar MySQL server is available on the MariaDB website.

qa_pairs.question: What is the general description of the Cedar Postgres server?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar Postgres server is a general purpose server for researchers wanting to set up SQL tables and issue SQL commands against them. It includes a PostGIS extension for those needing to do geocoding.

qa_pairs.question: Does the Cedar Postgres server include any extensions?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the Cedar Postgres server includes a PostGIS extension available for those needing to do geocoding.

qa_pairs.question: What is the full server name for Cedar Postgres?
qa_pairs.answer: The full server name for Cedar Postgres is cedar-pgsql-vm.int.cedar.computecanada.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the short server name for Cedar Postgres?
qa_pairs.answer: The short server name for Cedar Postgres is cedar-pgsql-vm.

qa_pairs.question: What is the latest PostgreSQL version available on the Cedar Postgres server?
qa_pairs.answer: PostgreSQL version 16 is the latest version available on the Cedar Postgres server.

qa_pairs.question: What is the version of the PostGIS extension on the Cedar Postgres server?
qa_pairs.answer: The PostGIS extension on the Cedar Postgres server is version 3.3.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find documentation for the Cedar Postgres server?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation for the Cedar Postgres server can be found on the Postgres website and the PostGIS documentation website.

qa_pairs.question: What is the general description of the Graham MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: The Graham MySQL server is a general purpose server for researchers wanting to set up SQL tables and issue SQL commands against them.

qa_pairs.question: What is the server name for the Graham MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: The server name for the Graham MySQL server is cc-gra-dbaas1.sharcnet.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the IP address for the Graham MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: The IP address for the Graham MySQL server is 199.241.163.99.

qa_pairs.question: Is there a short server name for Graham MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: No, there is no short server name available for Graham MySQL (N/A).

qa_pairs.question: What is the latest MariaDB version available on the Graham MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: MariaDB version 11.5 is the latest version available on the Graham MySQL server.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find documentation for the Graham MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: Documentation for the Graham MySQL server is available on the MariaDB website.

qa_pairs.question: What database system does the Cedar MySQL server offer?
qa_pairs.answer: The Cedar MySQL server offers MariaDB.

qa_pairs.question: Is MariaDB on the Cedar MySQL server compatible with other MySQL flavours?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, MariaDB on the Cedar MySQL server is compatible with other flavours of MySQL.

qa_pairs.question: How does the MariaDB server on Cedar run?
qa_pairs.answer: The MariaDB server on Cedar runs as a VM called "cedar-mysql-vm".

qa_pairs.question: How can users connect to the Cedar MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can connect to the Cedar MySQL server through the Cedar head node (cedar.computecanada.ca), Cedar compute nodes, and the Cedar Portal.

qa_pairs.question: Can users directly SSH into the Cedar MySQL database server?
qa_pairs.answer: No, for security reasons, users cannot make an SSH connection to the database server directly.

qa_pairs.question: What is required to create your own database on the Cedar MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: If you need the privileges to create your own database, you will need a MySQL account.

qa_pairs.question: How do I request a MySQL account on the Cedar MySQL server?
qa_pairs.answer: To get a MySQL account on the Cedar MySQL server, you need to send a request to Technical support with your Alliance username and the amount of database space needed for your project.

qa_pairs.question: What information is needed when requesting a Cedar MySQL account?
qa_pairs.answer: When requesting a Cedar MySQL account, you need to provide your Alliance username and the amount of database space needed for your project.

qa_pairs.question: What username will be assigned to a Cedar MySQL account?
qa_pairs.answer: An MySQL account will be created with the same username as your Alliance username.

qa_pairs.question: What kind of password is provided for a new Cedar MySQL account?
qa_pairs.answer: A 16-digit random string password will be created for a new Cedar MySQL account.

qa_pairs.question: Where is the Cedar MySQL connection information stored?
qa_pairs.answer: The username, password, database server name, and other information required to make a MySQL connection will be stored in a file called `.my.cnf` in your home directory.

qa_pairs.question: What are the restrictions on the `.my.cnf` file for Cedar MySQL?
qa_pairs.answer: The `.my.cnf` file is confidential; you cannot change its contents but you can read it or delete it.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if the `.my.cnf` file is deleted?
qa_pairs.answer: If the `.my.cnf` file is deleted, you will lose access to your database.

qa_pairs.question: How do I connect to the Cedar MySQL server using the client?
qa_pairs.answer: You run the "mysql" client to connect to the MySQL server.

qa_pairs.question: Is it recommended to load a module for the MySQL client on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is recommended to load a more recent version of the client using a module to access the latest features on the MySQL server.

qa_pairs.question: What commands should I use to load the recommended MySQL client version on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: You should use `module load mariadb` and then `mariadb --version` to load and verify a more recent version of the client.

qa_pairs.question: How can I test if my Cedar MySQL database account is set up correctly?
qa_pairs.answer: You can test your MySQL database account by running `mysql`, then typing `show databases;` and `quit`.

qa_pairs.question: Should I use the `-p` or `-h` options when running the `mysql` client on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you should not use the `-p` or `-h` options as arguments when running `mysql`, as the required password and server name are taken automatically from your `.my.cnf` file.

qa_pairs.question: How does the `mysql` client get the password and server name on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: The required password and server name are taken automatically from your `.my.cnf` file.

qa_pairs.question: Can I submit long-running SQL commands from the Cedar head node?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, it is acceptable to submit a long-running SQL command from the head node, as most of the CPU usage is taken from the database server side.

qa_pairs.question: What should I do if my SQL script uses a lot of CPU on Cedar?
qa_pairs.answer: If you run a script which issues SQL commands and uses lots of CPU, then it needs to be submitted as a job to the scheduler.
